item.title: In the light of all the madness I thought I'd share my story
item.description: In 2017 my wife and I were blessed with a child after about 6 months of trying. He was a very challenging pregnancy and my wife spent most of the 9 months being sick and fainting but he was worth it, especially for me as I had the easy part. Fast forward a year and we decided we wanted to try and have another one as I've always wanted 2 or 3 fairly close in age. We get pregnant fairly easily this time and all is going as expected until the 12 week scan when the ultrasound person goes really quiet and leaves the room to fetch senior midwives. After a barrage of tests, spread out over 10 weeks with just enough time for hope to build before it gets crushed again they are unable to tell us what condition our baby has but it is clearly incompatible with life as the baby is unable to move and not developing certain organs, primarily lungs, properly. We had to take a pill to stop our beautiful Aurora's heart and go in for her birth at the hospital. Leaving her behind when we left and went home was the worst thing I've ever had to do. They thought it was random gene anomaly and gave us a 5% chance of it happening again so a year later we decided to try. The same thing happened again with zephyr. During the 18 months the improvement in genome technology they were able to tell us what the issue was. By random chance the wife and I share a wonky gene, everyone has some and we just happened to have the same one. This condition now has a name and is always fatal as far as I'm aware. Now fortunately we're not going through this in the current climate of the USA (we're British so not impacted in the same way but this whole thing is horrific and scary). In the US as of now my wife would have had to carry our children to full term knowing they would never survive and the grief that would generate. I love all 3 of my children and would have done anything to have brought all of them home with me. I'm sorry for what you all are going through over there. Sorry its so long. Love you all ❤️
item.dc:creator: IAmTheKingOfCheese

item.title: Bully Cries Bully

item.dc:creator: Castironpeppers

item.title: I’m Hopeful but Terrified
item.description: I’ve been a quiet fence-sitter all my life, taking time to formulate my own opinions. However, putting my views out on a pedestal in public terrifies me. What if I get sent to jail or ostracized? I’m about to look for a job. What if my appearance there negatively affects my ability to find work? I live in a red state in the Bible Belt. That said, I think this cause may be worth it.
item.dc:creator: thethingthatwentsplat

item.title: Friend to everyone. Made history. Quietly retired to chillin' forever. Never went to space or tried to take over anything. Absolute fuckin' legend.
item.dc:creator: MarcMorg

item.title: Depressed, have some memes.
item.description:
I absolutely "love" how I show up on time to my web appointment, only for it to come and go without my counselor even showing up. I waited around and made sure everything was in order ( i.e camera and mic were working. Everything was filled out etc..) And this is all after barely sleeping, and quickly choking down my meds this morning. Awesome feeling, just freaking great. Good morning to me.
I need an unhealthy amount of coffee right now.
item.dc:creator: sleepyvoidboi
